summary: Backend + frontend testing of OmniLegal v2 multi-jurisdiction Chainlit console. Chainlit UI (PRIMARY interface) is fully functional: 9,972 indexed source passages (>7,000 target), correct title, 5 personas (Tourist/Law Student/Researcher/Layman/Conflict Detector), Quick triggers panel, Tourist query returns Sources panel + Citation audit + [S#] citations in ~20s, /conflict slash command returns a full Cross-Jurisdiction Conflict Report in ~50s with per-jurisdiction sections, rationale spans, color-coded verdicts (Israel labeled 🔴 Conflict, Russia 🟠 Qualified alignment), and VCLT Art. 27 reminder. The FastAPI sidecar on :8001, however, is broken for any corpus-dependent endpoint: /api/health passes but /api/ingestion/status returns total_points=0, /api/debug/retrieve returns 0 passages for STATUTES_IN, and /api/conflict/analyze returns structurally valid JSON in ~3s but with all per_jurisdiction entries showing 'No domestic authority retrieved' (domestic_passages=[]). Root cause: the subprocess scripts spawned by the sidecar (scripts/print_status.py, scripts/run_conflict.py) cannot open the embedded Qdrant store because Chainlit holds /app/data/qdrant_embedded/.lock. The subprocess emits 'Storage folder ... is already accessed by another instance of Qdrant client. Using explicit SQLite fallback' and falls back to an empty SQLite store. Chainlit owns the Qdrant lock (verified via /proc/4776/fd) and therefore in-process /conflict works, but the HTTP sidecar will keep returning empty responses as long as Chainlit is up.

critical.issue: Returns total_points=0 and every collection at 0 points. Subprocess script (scripts/print_status.py) cannot acquire the embedded-Qdrant lock because Chainlit owns it; falls back to empty SQLite store.

Returns 200 with valid schema in ~3s, but every per_jurisdiction entry has domestic_passages=[] and label='neutral' regardless of query (tested with Israel/US Palestinian-territories query and India/US death-penalty query). Subprocess scripts/run_conflict.py cannot read the corpus while Chainlit holds the lock; payload is essentially placeholder data.

action_items: Fix Qdrant lock contention for the FastAPI sidecar. Options: (a) switch the embedded Qdrant to a Qdrant server (HTTP) so multiple processes can share access; (b) have the sidecar import Chainlit's already-open client via an in-process IPC instead of spawning a subprocess; (c) document that /api/* endpoints only work when Chainlit is stopped and skip them in CI.

Backend log shows 'FlagEmbedding unavailable (No module named FlagEmbedding); falling back to FastEmbed for dense embeddings' — non-blocking but worth installing FlagEmbedding to honor the configured BAAI/bge-m3 dense model and BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3 reranker.

Cosmetic: the /conflict report sections are rendered as 'Jurisdiction — Verdict' headers with prose rationale spans rather than as a single markdown comparison table. Review request asked for 'a per-jurisdiction markdown table'. Consider rendering an explicit markdown table at the top of the report (Jurisdiction | Verdict | Confidence | Conflict?) before the per-section deep dive.
critical_code_review_comments: Architecture flaw in /app/backend/server.py: every corpus-dependent endpoint spawns a Python subprocess that re-opens the embedded Qdrant store (qdrant_client.QdrantClient(path=...)). Embedded Qdrant is single-process by design — when Chainlit is running (which is the documented production state of this app), every subprocess will fail to attach and will silently fall back to SQLite, returning empty results. The 'subprocess to avoid lock contention' comment in the agent_to_agent_context_note is the opposite of what actually happens. The sidecar must either share Chainlit's Qdrant client or speak to a real Qdrant server.

/api/conflict/analyze swallows the underlying retrieval failure: it returns 200 with verdict='neutral_or_unknown' and explanation='No retrieved authority tagged as <jur> matched the query', which is indistinguishable from a real 'no authority found' result. Recommend surfacing the subprocess stderr (e.g. 'embedded Qdrant locked, falling back to empty store') as a top-level diagnostic so headless callers can detect this misconfiguration.

chainlit_app.py _handle_conflict_command correctly calls analyze_multi_jurisdiction_conflict in-process via asyncio.to_thread, bypassing the subprocess — so the Chainlit slash command works while the HTTP API does not. This means the two surfaces have diverged: the same query against the same corpus returns very different results depending on whether you call /api/conflict or /conflict in chat.

Conflict report renderer (_render_conflict_payload, line ~191) emits prose per-jurisdiction blocks but no comparison table, while the review request and the docstring of the /irac command both promise a markdown comparison table. Consider unifying.

success_rate.backend: 40% (2/5 pytest tests pass — health + conflict structural shape; ingestion/retrieve/conflict-data all fail due to Qdrant lock)
success_rate.frontend: 100% of all 9 reviewed Chainlit checkpoints pass

test_credentials: N/A — Chainlit + sidecar are unauthenticated in preview
seed_data_creation: None — used the pre-bootstrapped 9,972-chunk embedded Qdrant corpus.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: https://b76f2c97-40e5-4b67-bcd2-eac077eac1a3.preview.emergentagent.com (Chainlit on 3000, FastAPI on 8001, both reachable via /api/* path). Chainlit process is python run_chainlit.py PID-style entry; it holds the embedded Qdrant lock at /app/data/qdrant_embedded/.lock — verified with /proc/<pid>/fd. To re-test the FastAPI corpus endpoints you'd need to stop Chainlit first, or the main agent must switch to a real Qdrant server. Chainlit slash commands: /conflict <q> takes ~45–60s end-to-end and returns per-jurisdiction blocks with color-coded verdict labels and VCLT Art.27 reminder; /irac and /verify were not exercised in this iteration but the same handler scaffolding exists at chainlit_app.py:347 and chainlit_app.py:358. Default persona = Tourist; persona dropdown is the compass icon at top-left and lists Tourist, Law Student, Researcher, Layman, Conflict Detector. Use textarea#chat-input + page.keyboard.press('Enter') to submit. The Tourist normal query test uses ~20s, the /conflict test uses ~50s — set browser timeouts to >=240s and poll body text for 'Conflict Report' / 'Verdict' / 'Persona:' markers.
rca of the issue: Embedded Qdrant (qdrant_client with local path) acquires an exclusive file lock on /app/data/qdrant_embedded/.lock. Chainlit boots first and holds this lock for its lifetime. The FastAPI sidecar's /api/ingestion/status, /api/debug/retrieve and /api/conflict/analyze endpoints implement themselves by spawning Python subprocesses (scripts/print_status.py, scripts/run_debug_retrieve.py, scripts/run_conflict.py) that each try to open the same embedded Qdrant path. The qdrant-client library raises 'Storage folder is already accessed by another instance of Qdrant client', the code catches this, prints 'Using explicit SQLite fallback', and proceeds with an empty fallback store. Repro: with Chainlit up, run `python /app/scripts/print_status.py` — total_points=0; stop Chainlit and re-run — corpus shows 9,972 points. Mitigation: (1) run Qdrant as a separate HTTP server (qdrant/qdrant container) and point both Chainlit and the sidecar at QDRANT_URL=http://localhost:6333; (2) replace the subprocess pattern with a small in-process FastAPI router served by Chainlit itself; or (3) gate the /api/* endpoints behind a 'chainlit-not-running' check and document them as offline-only diagnostics. Option 1 is the canonical fix and matches the .env hint OMNILEGAL_VECTOR_BACKEND=embedded_qdrant + QDRANT_URL=http://localhost:6333.
